
### revisted: within object  {.smaller}


```
struct foo {
    char buffer[1024];
    int *pointer;
};
struct foo array_of_foos[1024];
...
char *p = &array_of_foos[4].buffer[4]

```
 

* exercise: What out-of-bounds accesses to ‘p’ can AddressSanitizer detect?
* how does that compare to baggy bounds? To the ‘fat pointers’ strategy?
